english: What is the best transport for this place?
scenario: You ask a host or local before leaving.
goal: What is the best transport for this place?
possibleMoroccanReply: taksi wla tram, 3la hsab fin ghadi
cultureNote: The best option depends on distance, luggage, and time.

english: Should I go by tram or by taxi?
goal: Should I go by tram or by taxi?
cultureNote: A direct comparison question is practical and natural.

english: How long will it take?
scenario: You need to know travel time.
goal: How long will it take?
cultureNote: Time estimates may be approximate, so stay flexible.

english: Is there an easy route?
scenario: You have bags and want an easier way.
goal: Is there an easy route?
cultureNote: Say sahla when comfort matters more than speed.

english: Okay, I will take this one.
scenario: You decide which option to take.
goal: Okay, I will take this one.
cultureNote: Safi naturally closes a decision.

english: A small taxi to the city, please.
scenario: You are near the street and need a petit taxi.
goal: A small taxi to the city, please.
cultureNote: Petit taxis usually work inside city limits.

english: I want to go to this place.
scenario: You show a destination on your phone.
goal: I want to go to this place.
cultureNote: Showing the map helps if pronunciation is hard.

english: Is the meter working?
scenario: You want to avoid confusion about the fare.
goal: Is the meter working?
cultureNote: In city taxis, asking about the meter can be normal.

english: Are we close?
scenario: You are in the taxi and want to know if you are near.
goal: Are we close?
cultureNote: A very short question is enough in a taxi.

english: Here is good, thank you.
scenario: You reached a convenient stopping point.
goal: Here is good, thank you.
cultureNote: Short polite endings work well with drivers.

english: Where is the grand taxi?
scenario: You look for shared taxis near a station.
goal: Where is the grand taxi?
cultureNote: Grand taxis often have dedicated stands.

english: Are you going to that city?
scenario: You ask if the taxi goes to your destination.
goal: Are you going to that city?
cultureNote: Always confirm the city before entering.

english: How much is one seat?
scenario: You need the shared taxi seat price.
goal: How much is one seat?
cultureNote: Shared taxis may charge per seat.

english: Do I need to wait until it is full?
scenario: You want to know when the taxi leaves.
goal: Do I need to wait until it is full?
cultureNote: Shared taxis may wait for passengers before departure.

english: Good, this is my seat.
goal: Good, this is my seat.
cultureNote: Seat clarity avoids confusion.

english: Where is the train station?
scenario: You are in the city and need the station.
goal: Where is the train station?
cultureNote: L-gar is widely understood for train station.

english: Where is the ticket office?
scenario: You enter the station and need a ticket.
goal: Where is the ticket office?
cultureNote: French loan words are common in transport contexts.

english: I want to see the schedules.
goal: I want to see the schedules.
cultureNote: Stations often show schedules on screens.

english: Where is the platform?
scenario: You have a ticket and need the platform.
goal: Where is the platform?
cultureNote: Platform words may be in French.

english: Thanks, I will follow the signs.
goal: Thanks, I will follow the signs.
cultureNote: Signs help, but asking once can save time.

english: I want a ticket to Rabat, please.
scenario: You are at the ticket counter.
goal: I want a ticket to Rabat, please.
cultureNote: Use city names clearly at stations.

english: Today or tomorrow?
scenario: The agent asks about the date.
goal: Today or tomorrow?

english: I want a ticket today in the evening.
goal: I want a ticket today in the evening.
cultureNote: Moroccan time expressions like l-3shiya are useful.

english: Is there a seat available?
goal: Is there a seat available?
cultureNote: Availability matters on busy routes.

english: I got the ticket, thank you.
scenario: The ticket is printed or confirmed.
goal: I got the ticket, thank you.
cultureNote: Check city and time before leaving the counter.

english: Which platform is the train on?
goal: Which platform is the train on?

english: Is this the train to Casablanca?
scenario: A train arrives and you want to confirm.
goal: Is this the train to Casablanca?
cultureNote: Confirm before boarding.

english: How long until departure?
scenario: You are waiting on the platform.
goal: How long until departure?
cultureNote: Departure terms may be in French.

english: Where is my seat on the train?
scenario: You board and look for your seat.
goal: Where is my seat on the train?
cultureNote: Wagon and seat numbers matter.

english: This is my seat, right?
goal: This is my seat, right?
cultureNote: A polite confirmation avoids conflict.

english: Is the train delayed?
goal: Is the train delayed?
cultureNote: Delays can happen; stay calm and ask staff.

english: How long will it be?
goal: How long will it be?
cultureNote: Estimates may change.

english: Did the platform change?
goal: Did the platform change?
cultureNote: Platform changes require quick calm action.

english: Do I need to change my ticket?
goal: Do I need to change my ticket?

english: Okay, I will wait here.
scenario: You decide to wait after clarification.
goal: Okay, I will wait here.
cultureNote: Clear decisions reduce travel stress.

english: Where is the bus station?

goal: Where is the bus station?

english: Is this CTM?

goal: Is this CTM?
cultureNote: Company names matter for buses.

english: I want a ticket to Agadir.
scenario: You are at the bus counter.
goal: I want a ticket to Agadir.
cultureNote: Use destination clearly and slowly.

friendlyLatin: f ash mn l-waqt kayn l-kar?
english: What time is the bus?
goal: What time is the bus?
cultureNote: Bus times should be checked carefully.
pronunciationParts: f ash mn l-waqt / kayn l-kar

english: Where do I board?

goal: Where do I board?

english: I want a front seat if possible.
scenario: You prefer a front seat on the bus.
goal: I want a front seat if possible.
cultureNote: Seat preferences are requests, not guarantees.

english: Is luggage included in the price?
goal: Is luggage included in the price?
cultureNote: Baggage rules vary, so ask before boarding.

english: I want a one-way ticket.
scenario: You do not need a return ticket.
goal: I want a one-way ticket.
cultureNote: French ticket terms may be understood.

english: Can I change the time?
scenario: You may need a different departure.
goal: Can I change the time?

english: Give me the ticket, please.
goal: Give me the ticket, please.

english: Where do I put the luggage?

goal: Where do I put the luggage?

english: This is my bag.
goal: This is my bag.

english: Do I need a luggage ticket?
goal: Do I need a luggage ticket?

english: Please unload the luggage here.

goal: Please unload the luggage here.
cultureNote: Be polite but clear when retrieving bags.

english: Thanks, this is mine.
goal: Thanks, this is mine.

english: Where is the tram station?
scenario: You need the nearest tram stop.
goal: Where is the tram station?

english: Is this the direction to the center?

goal: Is this the direction to the center?

english: Where do I buy a ticket?
goal: Where do I buy a ticket?

english: Do I need to validate the ticket?
scenario: You have a ticket or card.
goal: Do I need to validate the ticket?

english: Where do I get off?
goal: Where do I get off?

english: Where is transport to the city center?
goal: Where is transport to the city center?

english: Is there a train from the airport?
goal: Is there a train from the airport?

english: How much is a taxi to the center?
goal: How much is a taxi to the center?

english: I want to go to the hotel from the airport.
scenario: You tell the airport taxi your destination.
goal: I want to go to the hotel from the airport.

english: Okay, we will take a taxi.
goal: Okay, we will take a taxi.

english: I want to go from the station to the riad.
scenario: You exit the station with luggage.

english: Is it close from here?

goal: Is it close from here?

english: Can I walk?
scenario: You want to avoid taxi if nearby.
goal: Can I walk?
cultureNote: Walking may be possible but not with heavy bags.

english: I have luggage; what is best?
goal: I have luggage; what is best?

english: Take me to the riad door, please.
scenario: You need door to door help.
goal: Take me to the riad door, please.

english: Where is the main exit?
goal: Where is the main exit?

english: Where is the parking?
goal: Where is the parking?

english: Is there an ATM nearby?
goal: Is there an ATM nearby?

english: Please repeat slowly for me.
goal: Please repeat slowly for me.

english: I understand, thank you very much.
goal: I understand, thank you very much.

english: After the train, I need a taxi.
goal: After the train, I need a taxi.

goal: Are taxis outside?

english: I want to go to the old medina.

arabic: من هنا غادي نمشي على رجلي
english: From here I will walk.
goal: From here I will walk.

english: I want to go from Marrakech to Fes.

english: What is better, train or bus?
goal: What is better, train or bus?

english: How many hours?

english: Do I need a reservation?
scenario: You ask if booking is needed.
goal: Do I need a reservation?

english: Better I do it now.
goal: Better I do it now.
cultureNote: Make the decision while information is clear.

english: Is this seat empty?
goal: Is this seat empty?

english: Can I sit here?
goal: Can I sit here?

english: Please, a little to the side.
goal: Please, a little to the side.

english: I do not want to bother you.
scenario: You are moving luggage or sitting.
goal: I do not want to bother you.

english: This is enough for me here.

goal: This is enough for me here.

goal: Excuse me, did you understand the place?

english: I will show you on the phone.

english: This is the address.

english: Okay, let us go.

pronunciationParts: sma7 lia / kan khasni / nhbet qbel

english: Can I go back from here?

english: Where is the nearest stop?

english: No problem, thank you.
scenario: You accept the mistake and continue.
goal: No problem, thank you.

english: I have a big suitcase.

english: Is there space for the suitcase?

english: Help me lift it, please.
goal: Help me lift it, please.

goal: Should I leave it here?

arabic: ما بغيتش نمشي على رجلي بالليل

goal: Call me a taxi, please.

english: I will wait here.
scenario: You wait in a safe place.
goal: I will wait here.

english: We are two people.

english: We want seats next to each other.

english: We have a small child.

english: I only have 100.
goal: I only have 100.

english: Take the fare.

english: How much change remains for me?

english: Send me the location, please.
goal: Send me the location, please.

english: I will come at six.
goal: I will come at six.

english: My name is on the reservation.
goal: My name is on the reservation.

arabic: إلا تبدل شي حاجة، عيط ليا

friendlyLatin: rje3 lia f had l-waqt afak

english: I had a problem on the way.
goal: I had a problem on the way.

english: I want to know what to do now.
